mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 shows consecutive quarterly growth but S&P 500 20d returns are negative in > 40% of cases, contradicting the positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% indicates monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% but S&P 500 5d returns are positive in > 50% of instances, invalidating the tightening impact.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI (CPIAUCSL) and rising FEDFUNDS signal disinflationary pressures and hawkish policy, creating headwinds for equity markets and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL declines and FEDFUNDS rises but S&P 500 60d returns are positive in > 60% of cases, refuting the interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% combined with rising payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates labor market strength, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE < 3.5% and PAYEMS growth persist but S&P 500 20d returns are negative in > 40% of cases, invalidating the labor-driven rally.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weak supply chain activity, which reduces corporate margins and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO falls below 100 but S&P 500 5d returns are positive in > 50% of instances, contradicting the supply-driven decline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.5 million units reflects robust demand for real estate, which boosts construction-related sectors and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 1500 but S&P 500 60d returns are negative in > 40% of cases, invalidating the housing demand effect.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in retail sales (RSAFS) below 650 billion dollars indicates weak consumer demand, which pressures corporate revenues and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS falls below 650 but S&P 500 20d returns are positive in > 50% of cases, refuting the demand-driven decline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer confidence and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE exceeds 4.0% but S&P 500 5d returns are positive in > 50% of instances, invalidating the labor market impact.
